name: redis.uptime
definition: Number of seconds since Redis server start. Cumulative counter useful for detecting restarts (resets to 0).
absence_means: Instance not reporting or collector misconfigured

name: redis.clients.connected
definition: Number of client connections excluding replicas. Non-monotonic sum (gauge-like). Key saturation metric when compared to maxclients.
absence_means: Instance down or no clients connected

name: redis.clients.blocked
definition: Number of clients pending on a blocking call (BLPOP, BRPOP, WAIT, XREAD BLOCK). Non-monotonic sum.
absence_means: No clients in blocking operations (normal)

name: redis.connections.rejected
definition: Number of connections rejected because of maxclients limit. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: No connection rejections (healthy) or instance not reporting

name: redis.connections.received
definition: Total number of connections accepted by the server. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: Instance not reporting

name: redis.slaves.connected
definition: Number of connected replicas. Non-monotonic sum. Value 0 means standalone or all replicas disconnected.
absence_means: Standalone mode (no replication) or replicas disconnected

name: redis.commands.processed
definition: Total number of commands processed by the server. Cumulative monotonic counter. Preferred for throughput calculation over redis.commands.
absence_means: Instance not reporting

name: redis.commands
definition: Instantaneous ops/sec gauge snapshot from Redis INFO. NOT a counter. Do NOT apply rate(). Noisy sampled value updated every 100ms internally.
absence_means: Instance not reporting
cardinality_footguns: Noisy single-point sample; prefer rate(redis.commands.processed) for accurate throughput

name: redis.keyspace.hits
definition: Number of successful lookup of keys in the main dictionary. Cumulative monotonic counter. Input for cache hit ratio.
absence_means: No read operations or instance not reporting

name: redis.keyspace.misses
definition: Number of failed lookup of keys in the main dictionary. Cumulative monotonic counter. Input for cache hit ratio.
absence_means: No read operations or instance not reporting

name: redis.keys.expired
definition: Total number of key expiration events. Cumulative monotonic counter. High rate may cause latency from active expiry cycle.
absence_means: No keys with TTL or instance not reporting

name: redis.keys.evicted
definition: Number of evicted keys due to maxmemory limit. Cumulative monotonic counter. For caches eviction is normal; for data stores it means data loss.
absence_means: Memory below maxmemory or noeviction policy configured

name: redis.net.input
definition: Total number of bytes read from the network. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: Instance not reporting

name: redis.net.output
definition: Total number of bytes written to the network. Cumulative monotonic counter.
absence_means: Instance not reporting

name: redis.memory.used
definition: Total number of bytes allocated by Redis using its allocator. Gauge. Primary memory consumption metric.
absence_means: Instance not reporting

name: redis.memory.peak
definition: Peak memory consumed by Redis (in bytes). Gauge. High-water mark since last restart.
absence_means: Instance not reporting

name: redis.memory.rss
definition: Number of bytes that Redis allocated as seen by the OS (resident set size). Gauge. Includes fragmentation overhead, COW pages during fork, and allocator metadata.
absence_means: Instance not reporting

name: redis.memory.lua
definition: Number of bytes used by the Lua engine. Gauge.
absence_means: No Lua scripts loaded

name: redis.memory.fragmentation_ratio
definition: Ratio of used_memory_rss to used_memory. <1.0 = likely swapping. 1.0-1.5 = healthy. >1.5 = fragmentation. Gauge. Can be misleading: includes non-fragmentation overhead (shared libs, allocator metadata).
absence_means: Instance not reporting
cardinality_footguns: Misleading when Redis uses very little memory (ratio can be very high due to fixed overhead)

name: redis.maxmemory
definition: Value of the maxmemory configuration directive. Gauge. 0 = unlimited (64-bit default). Denominator for memory utilization calculations. OTel: disabled by default, must enable.
absence_means: maxmemory not configured (unlimited) or OTel metric not enabled. When 0, cannot compute utilization %.

name: redis.db.keys
definition: Number of keyspace keys per database. Gauge. Attributed by db (db0-db15).
absence_means: Database is empty or does not exist
cardinality_footguns: Per-db cardinality: up to 16 databases x N instances. Most deployments use db0 only.

name: redis.db.expires
definition: Number of keyspace keys with an expiration per database. Gauge. Attributed by db.
absence_means: No keys with TTL set in this database

name: redis.db.avg_ttl
definition: Average keyspace keys TTL per database. Gauge. Attributed by db. Unit is milliseconds.
absence_means: No keys with TTL set in this database

name: redis.cpu.time
definition: System CPU consumed by Redis in seconds since server start. Cumulative monotonic counter. Attributed by state (sys, user, sys_children, user_children, sys_main_thread, user_main_thread). rate() gives CPU seconds/second (utilization).
absence_means: Instance not reporting
cardinality_footguns: state attribute has 6 values (sys, user, sys_children, user_children, sys_main_thread, user_main_thread)

name: redis.clients.max_input_buffer
definition: Biggest input buffer among current client connections. Gauge. Bytes.
absence_means: No clients connected or instance not reporting

name: redis.clients.max_output_buffer
definition: Longest output list among current client connections. Gauge. Bytes.
absence_means: No clients connected or instance not reporting

name: redis.latest_fork
definition: Duration of the latest fork operation in microseconds. Gauge. Spikes during BGSAVE/BGREWRITEAOF. Proportional to dataset size (9-13us/MB on physical hardware).
absence_means: No fork has occurred (persistence disabled or no rewrite yet)

name: redis.cmd.calls
definition: Total number of calls for a specific command. Cumulative monotonic counter. Attributed by cmd. OTel: disabled by default, must enable.
absence_means: OTel metric not enabled, or no commands of this type executed
cardinality_footguns: HIGH cardinality: 200+ commands with modules. Always use Top-N limit on cmd dimension.

name: redis.cmd.usec
definition: Total time in microseconds for all executions of a command. Cumulative monotonic counter. Attributed by cmd. OTel: disabled by default, must enable.
absence_means: OTel metric not enabled or no commands of this type executed

name: redis.cmd.latency
definition: Command execution latency. Gauge in seconds. Attributed by cmd and percentile (p50, p99, p99.9). OTel: disabled by default. Requires Redis 7.0+ with latency-tracking yes.
absence_means: latency-tracking not enabled (Redis 7.0+), or Redis < 7.0, or OTel metric not enabled
cardinality_footguns: HIGH cardinality: cmd x percentile x instances. Use Top-N on cmd.

name: redis.rdb.changes_since_last_save
definition: Number of changes since the last RDB dump. Non-monotonic sum. Climbs during normal operation, resets to 0 after successful save.
absence_means: Persistence disabled (save "") -- metric climbs indefinitely

name: redis.replication.offset
definition: The server's current replication offset. Gauge. On master, this is master_repl_offset. Used to compute replication lag vs replica offset.
absence_means: Standalone mode or replication not configured

name: redis.replication.backlog_first_byte_offset
definition: The master offset of the replication backlog buffer. Gauge.
absence_means: Replication not active

name: redis.replication.replica_offset
definition: Offset for redis replica. Gauge. OTel: disabled by default, must enable. Used with master offset to compute replication lag.
absence_means: Not a replica or OTel metric not enabled

name: redis.memory.used_memory_overhead
definition: Sum of all overheads that the server allocated for managing internal data structures. Non-monotonic sum. OTel: disabled by default, must enable.
absence_means: OTel metric not enabled

name: redis.memory.used_memory_startup
definition: Initial amount of memory consumed by Redis at startup. Non-monotonic sum. OTel: disabled by default, must enable.
absence_means: OTel metric not enabled

name: redis.role
definition: Redis node's role. Non-monotonic sum. Attributed by role (primary, replica). OTel: disabled by default, must enable.
absence_means: OTel metric not enabled

name: redis.mode
definition: Redis server mode. Gauge. Attributed by mode (standalone, sentinel, cluster). OTel: disabled by default, must enable.
absence_means: OTel metric not enabled

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: Redis is self-hosted on k8s. There are no direct Redis cost metrics. Cost proxies: redis.memory.used (memory is the primary resource), redis.cpu.time (CPU consumption), redis.net.input+redis.net.output (network). Infrastructure cost is tracked at the k8s/host level, not within Redis telemetry.
